> <details>
> <summary>⌛ How to resolve this issue?</summary>
> 
> After the wait time has elapsed, a review can be triggered using the `@coderabbitai review` command as a PR comment. Alternatively, push new commits to this PR.
> 
> We recommend that you space out your commits to avoid hitting the rate limit.
> 
> </details>
> 
> 
> <details>
> <summary>🚦 How do rate limits work?</summary>
> 
> CodeRabbit enforces hourly rate limits for each developer per organization.
> 
> Our paid plans have higher rate limits than the trial, open-source and free plans. In all cases, we re-allow further reviews after a brief timeout.
> 
> Please see our [FAQ](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/faq) for further information.

> </details>

performed_via_github_app.name: coderabbitai
performed_via_github_app.description:
# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues

</details>

body:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces cron-based scheduled job ("job tick") infrastructure for Coworker V2, including a `worker_jobs` DB table, a full service/DB layer, BullMQ queue integration, a 60-second sync loop that keeps BullMQ repeatables in sync with DB state, a tick processor that sends check-in prompts via the gateway, and a complete Settings-tab UI for CRUD operations on jobs. Key issues found: - **Critical — static idempotency key in `processor.ts`**: The `idempotencyKey` sent to the gateway is `worker-job-tick:{jobId}`, which is the same on every execution for a given job. If the gateway's deduplication TTL outlives the cron interval, all ticks after the first will be silently dropped and no check-in prompts will be delivered. It should be scoped to the individual BullMQ job execution (e.g. `worker-job-tick:{jobId}:{job.id}`). - **Worker status type mismatch in `worker-list-row.tsx`**: The local `WorkerStatus` type uses `"paused"` instead of the canonical `"automations_paused"`. Workers in the paused state will render with an `undefined` status dot and an empty label badge at runtime. - **No client-side cron validation in `WorkerJobForm`**: The form only checks that the `cronExpression` field is non-empty; invalid expressions are only rejected after a server round-trip, giving users no immediate feedback.

Confidence Score: 2/5

- Not safe to merge — the static idempotency key will cause the core tick delivery feature to silently stop working after the first execution per job. - The critical idempotency key bug in the processor means the primary feature of this PR (delivering scheduled check-in prompts) is broken for any job after its very first tick, depending on the gateway's deduplication TTL. The worker list row status mismatch is a visible regression for paused coworkers. These two issues together require fixes before the feature can be relied upon. - apps/trigger-service/src/job-ticks/processor.ts (idempotency key bug) and apps/web/src/components/automations/worker-list-row.tsx (status type mismatch)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | apps/trigger-service/src/job-ticks/processor.ts | New tick processor with a critical logic bug: the idempotency key is static per-job rather than per-execution, which will silently suppress all check-in prompts after the first tick if the gateway's deduplication TTL exceeds the cron interval. | | apps/trigger-service/src/job-ticks/scheduler.ts | New BullMQ scheduler that syncs enabled jobs every 60s. Correctly handles add/remove/update of repeatables, but does not detect stale managerSessionId when cron expression is unchanged (noted in a prior thread). | | packages/services/src/worker-jobs/service.ts | Well-structured service layer with create/update/delete/find/list operations and cron validation. The public listAllEnabledJobs returns the raw WorkerJobRow type instead of the mapped WorkerJobDetail, breaking the abstraction layer (flagged in a prior thread). | | apps/web/src/server/routers/automations.ts | Five new ORPC procedures for worker job CRUD; all use service-level functions that throw typed errors, which are correctly caught and mapped. The getWorkerJob handler delegates to the service which throws on not-found, so no null-return risk at the router level. | | apps/web/src/components/automations/worker-list-row.tsx | Adds description and lastWakeAt display. Contains a status type mismatch: local type uses "paused" instead of the canonical "automations_paused", so paused workers render with undefined status dot and label. | | apps/web/src/components/automations/worker-settings-tab.tsx | Well-structured settings tab with job CRUD UI; correctly uses confirmation dialogs for destructive actions and debounced saves for objective field. | | packages/queue/src/index.ts | Adds createWorkerJobTickQueue and createWorkerJobTickWorker factories following established patterns; job options (2 attempts, fixed 5s backoff) are reasonable for best-effort tick delivery. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant DB as PostgreSQL participant Scheduler as JobTickScheduler (60s loop) participant BullMQ as BullMQ (Redis) participant Worker as JobTickWorker participant Gateway as Gateway Scheduler->>DB: listAllEnabledJobs() Scheduler->>BullMQ: getRepeatableJobs() Scheduler->>BullMQ: add(job-tick:{jobId}, cronExpression) or removeRepeatableByKey() Note over BullMQ: Cron fires at scheduled time BullMQ->>Worker: processJobTick(job) Worker->>DB: workerJobs.findJobById(jobId) Worker->>DB: workers.findWorkerById(workerId) Worker->>Gateway: eagerStart(managerSessionId) Worker->>Gateway: postMessage(managerSessionId, checkInPrompt) Worker->>DB: updateJobTickTimestamps(lastTickAt, nextTickAt) ``` Last reviewed commit: a549071

performed_via_github_app.name: Greptile Apps
performed_via_github_app.description: Greptile is an AI agent that reviews pull requests with full context of your codebase. It can catch hidden bugs, security vulnerabilities, and anti-patterns.
